title: Extension surface is Why + Capture + Journey: one read screen, one write picker, one feel-the-compounding screen
context: After the v0.9.0 cut to Why + Capture, the owner reported the extension felt disconnected — clean but invisible; nothing let a user FEEL the memory compounding or their part in the process. Options considered: event toasts only (loop moments), a Journey panel, both, or restoring the old tree/home surfaces.
decision: The extension keeps exactly three explainable surfaces. READ: 'Hunch: Why is this?' (file brief + symbol section at the cursor), symbol hover, shield status-bar invariant counter. WRITE: one 'Hunch: Capture…' picker (decision via hunch mcp, invariant/bug via CLI). FEEL: 'Hunch: Journey' — one READ-ONLY webview: the memory curve drawn from real decision timestamps, the return line that moves only when a real gate blocks a real mistake, what the repo learned this week, and ONE suggested next action; a 🧠 decision-count status item is its front door. Plus 'Hunch: Search memory' and the three invisible language-model tools. The v0.9.0 litmus stands — each surface must be explainable in one sentence — and Journey passes: 'watch your repo's memory compound'. Journey computes nothing: numbers copied from the graph and hunch stats --json, zeros stay zeros (same honesty contract as the public proof curve). Everything deleted in v0.9.0 stays deleted; draft triage stays in the CLI.
consequences: Command palette shows 4 Hunch commands: Why, Capture, Journey, Search.
Two small status items (shield = file guards, 🧠 = memory count → Journey), both behind hunch.statusBar.enabled.
Journey is read-only forever; adding buttons/actions to it requires superseding this decision.
alternatives_rejected: Loop-moment toasts only — rejected for now: notifications without a place to look give a pulse but no story; may be ADDED later as a complement, not a substitute.
Restore the activity-bar tree or Home hub — rejected: that is the three-products-in-a-trenchcoat surface v0.9.0 deleted; browsing lives in CLI and search.
Put Journey behind the shield status item — rejected: the shield answers a file-scoped question (what guards THIS file); overloading it hides both answers.
